title: bus.agent.run.eval v1
description:
A SYNTHESIS EVALUATION — the verdict the Reflexarc synthesis pass (b7 / mgv7z) reaches about ONE recurring behavioral issue, plus the result of replaying a candidate deterministic fix against history. Emitted once per (issue_signature) per synthesis pass. Member of the bus.agent.* family — flat data-only; the CloudEvents-lite envelope is applied by `nervous publish`.

WHY THIS EXISTS: the Tier-1 detectors raise candidates into the issues/detector_hits tables (Kyoko prevalence + recurrence). Synthesis ranks those issues, climbs each to the highest Remediation-Ladder rung (Eliminate > Automate > Inform), and — critically — GATES a proposed fix behind a replay-eval: a counterfactual re-run proving the fix would have prevented the historical firings WITHOUT falsely suppressing legitimate behavior. run.eval is the durable record of that decision; a `decision==propose_fix` eval is what licenses emitting <project>.pattern.discovered.v1 (and optionally filing a proposal bead).

GROUNDED IN THE LIVE STREAM (2026-06-13): outcome labels are SPARSE (~10% of runs labeled; the rest close on idle_timeout unlabeled). Therefore synthesis MUST rank primarily on deterministic detector signal (prevalence x recurrence x ladder-rung), using outcome labels only as a refinement/confirmation boost — never as a gate. `labeled_support` carries the (small) labeled breakdown explicitly, with unlabeled counted as its own bucket, so a consumer can see how thin the label confirmation is. Runs that fragmented across idle_timeout are expected to be re-stitched (continues_run_id) BEFORE counting, so a single logical run is not double-counted in prevalence/recurrence.

eval_id.description: ULID for this evaluation record (sortable, monotonic, 26 chars). Primary key; a new eval is produced each synthesis pass that re-evaluates the signature.

project.description: Project the issue belongs to (repo dir name, or 'main'). The keying axis for cross-run learning, mirrored from the issue.

issue_signature.description: The stable issue signature being evaluated — '<project>:<detector>:<stable_anchor>', the issues-table primary key. Contains NO run_id and NO timestamp (that is what lets recurrence dedup work). This is the identity a fix is proposed against and that replay is gated on.

pattern_name.description: Human-readable pattern name (e.g. 'worktree_leak', 'rebuild_cache_miss'), mirrored from the detector candidate.

detector.description: Detector that raised the issue (DETECTOR_NAME). When multiple detectors corroborate one signature, the primary detector; see `corroborating_detectors`.

corroborating_detectors.description: Other detectors whose hits reinforce this issue (e.g. rebuild_cache_miss + worktree_leak both pointing at un-shared worktree state). Empty/absent when only one detector fired.

synthesis_pass_at.description: RFC3339 UTC — when this synthesis pass produced the eval.

score.description: Composite ranking score (higher = more worth fixing). A monotonic function of score_components; the absolute scale is internal but stable within a label_version of the scoring function. Used to order proposals.

score_components.description: TRANSPARENT breakdown of `score` so a human/auditor can see WHY an issue ranked. Synthesis must populate every component it used; a consumer can re-derive `score` from these. Keeps the ranking auditable rather than a black box.

prevalence.description: Kyoko #2: distinct hit runs / total runs for this (project, detector) over the scoring window (0..1).

recurrence_count.description: Kyoko #5: issues.recurrence_count — how many times this exact signature has recurred across runs.

ladder_rung_weight.description: Weight from the achievable remediation rung — Eliminate > Automate > Inform. Higher rungs score higher because they are both more valuable AND more measurable (replay-testable), which self-reinforces climbing the ladder.

label_confirmation.description: Refinement boost from sparse outcome labels: positive when labeled runs touching this issue skew toward failure outcomes (thrashed/abandoned/reverted), ~0 when unlabeled, slightly negative when labeled clean. NEVER a gate — labels are too sparse to gate on.

recency.description: Decay factor favoring issues seen recently (issues.last_seen) over stale ones.

window_days.description: Rolling window (days) over which prevalence/recency were computed.

labeled_support.description: EXPLICIT label breakdown of the runs touching this issue, gating on labeled_at (per the run.closed null-vs-clean rule). `unlabeled` is its own bucket and is expected to dominate — synthesis must NOT fold it into clean. Lets a consumer judge how thin the ground-truth confirmation is behind a proposal.

confirmed_failures.description: Runs labeled thrashed/abandoned/reverted that exhibit this issue.

confirmed_clean.description: Runs labeled clean/landed/corrected that exhibit this issue (a high count here is a SUPPRESSION signal — the behavior may be benign).

unlabeled.description: Runs exhibiting the issue that are not yet labeled (labeled_at IS NULL). Usually the majority; carries no outcome signal.

rung.description: The HIGHEST achievable Remediation-Ladder rung for this issue's fix, after synthesis climbed it. Eliminate = remove the root cause (e.g. share the cargo cache); Automate = a deterministic guard/skill/setting; Inform = surface to a human. Synthesis must climb as high as the evidence supports.

rung_descent_reason.description: If `rung` is below 'eliminate', the explicit reason the higher rung was not achievable (e.g. 'error class not captured in telemetry, cannot name a deterministic guard'). The Remediation-Ladder discipline: record WHY you fell. null when rung=='eliminate'.

remediation.description: The concrete proposed fix at `rung`. Mirrors/sharpens the detector's proposed_remediation. `target` is what would be changed if accepted.

proposal.description: Human-readable description of the deterministic fix.

target.description: Machine-actionable target of the fix — aligns with <project>.pattern.discovered.v1 proposed_patch.

type.description: Kind of change. 'env'/'config' typically Eliminate (e.g. CARGO_TARGET_DIR); 'skill'/'rule' typically Automate; 'doc' typically Inform.

selector.description: Where the change lands (file path, settings key, hook id, etc.).

replay_gate.description: THE AUTOMATE REPLAY-EVAL GATE — the measurable counterfactual that licenses (or blocks) a fix. Synthesis replays the candidate fix against historical runs and measures whether it would have prevented the issue WITHOUT suppressing legitimate behavior. This is what makes a proposal trustworthy and is the self-reinforcement of the ladder: Eliminate/Automate fixes are replay-testable; an Inform 'fix' is not measurable, so it cannot pass the gate — structurally biasing the system toward higher rungs.

status.description: passed = the simulated fix prevents the historical firings with acceptable false-suppression; failed = it does not prevent them OR suppresses legitimate runs (DO NOT propose); not_applicable = rung==inform (nothing deterministic to replay); insufficient_history = too few historical runs to evaluate; pending = gate not yet run.

method.description: How the counterfactual was evaluated (e.g. 're-ran the detector against N historical runs with CARGO_TARGET_DIR-shared simulated; counted firings eliminated').

runs_evaluated.description: Number of historical (re-stitched) runs the replay was evaluated against.

would_have_prevented.description: Counterfactual: how many historical firings of this issue the simulated fix would have eliminated.

false_suppression.description: Runs where the simulated fix would have wrongly suppressed/altered legitimate behavior. A non-trivial value FAILS the gate — guards against overfit fixes that 'work' by breaking valid cases.

prevention_rate.description: would_have_prevented / (firings in evaluated window). The headline measurable.

measurability_note.description: Why this rung is replay-measurable (or, for not_applicable, why it is not). Documents the self-reinforcement.

decision.description: The synthesis verdict. propose_fix = replay_gate passed -> emit <project>.pattern.discovered.v1 (+ optionally file a proposal bead). monitor = real but below the prevalence/recurrence bar to act. suppressed = replay_gate failed or confirmed_clean dominates -> explicitly DO NOT propose (the gate did its job). needs_more_data = insufficient_history / too few runs.

decision_rationale.description: Short justification tying score + replay_gate + labeled_support to the decision, so the call is auditable.

supersedes_eval_id.description: The prior eval_id for this issue_signature, if this pass re-evaluated it; null on first evaluation. Lets consumers track how a verdict evolved (e.g. monitor -> propose_fix as recurrence grew).

run_sample.description: A bounded sample of run_ids exhibiting the issue, for evidence/drill-down. Not exhaustive.

scorer_version.description: Monotonic version of the scoring+gate function that produced this eval. Lets `score` values be compared only within the same scorer_version, and old evals be re-scored when the function changes.

schema_version.description: Major schema version (semver major only, per repo convention).
